articles.title: Somerville restaurant owner says viral ‘pool water’ reply to customer review was unauthorized
articles.description:
The owner of Olivia's Kitchen said the reply "does not reflect our values" after screenshots surfaced on Reddit.

The post Somerville restaurant owner says viral ‘pool water’ reply to customer review was unauthorized appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Olivia's Kitchen owner disavowed viral response to a customer review on Reddit.
Owner stated the reply does not reflect the restaurant's values.
Incident highlights the importance of customer service in Somerville's dining scene.

articles.title: With split vote, City Council effort to reject Wu’s budget fails
articles.description:
Some councilors argued a rejection would send a powerful message to Mayor Wu, while others said it would be irresponsible.

The post With split vote, City Council effort to reject Wu’s budget fails appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: City Council voted 6-5 against rejecting Mayor Wu's budget proposal.
Some councilors argued rejection would send a message, while others deemed it irresponsible.
The decision maintains funding levels for Boston's city services amid ongoing budget discussions.

articles.title: Kevin McHale questions Celtics’ 3-point heavy approach: ‘The ball has got to touch the paint’
articles.description:
“Some days the 3-point shooting contest gets a little bit much for me and I do some head shaking,” McHale said.

summary: Kevin McHale critiques Celtics' reliance on 3-point shooting during recent games.

Celtics fans may see changes in gameplay as coaching staff responds to feedback.

articles.title: Mass. pols issue report on possible effects of Haiti TPS termination on local health care
articles.description:
The lawmakers' investigation found that terminating TPS would "exacerbate" shortages in the health care workforce, limit high-quality care, and "endanger patients," the report says.

The post Mass. pols issue report on possible effects of Haiti TPS termination on local health care appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Lawmakers report TPS termination could worsen health care workforce shortages in Massachusetts.
The investigation highlights potential risks to patient care quality and safety.
Local health care systems may face increased strain, affecting Boston residents' access to services.

articles.title: This New England town hosts one of America’s best 4th of July celebrations, according to USA Today readers

summary: Celebration in Bristol, Rhode Island, is the oldest continuous Fourth of July event in the U.S.

Boston residents can consider traveling to Bristol for a historic and festive experience.

articles.title: Stephen Colbert bids farewell to ‘Late Show’ with star-studded finale
articles.description:
Stephen Colbert hosted the final episode of "The Late Show" on Thursday night, welcoming Paul McCartney as his final guest and a number of celebrities to see him off.

summary: Stephen Colbert hosted his final 'Late Show' episode on May 22, 2026.

Colbert's departure may affect local viewership and late-night programming in Boston.

articles.title: ‘A train wreck’: Hampshire faculty, staff left with no jobs, no severance as college prepares for closure
articles.description:
NORTHAMPTON — When Gaurav Jashnani was offered a position as an assistant professor at Hampshire College, he saw it as a good move: Even though the iconoclastic liberal arts school doesn’t have a tenure system, the job would put him on a forward-moving track at a forward-looking institution. So in 2024, he relocated his family […]

The post ‘A train wreck’: Hampshire faculty, staff left with no jobs, no severance as college prepares for closure appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Hampshire College is closing, leaving faculty and staff without jobs or severance pay.
Gaurav Jashnani, an assistant professor, relocated his family for the position in 2024.
The closure affects local employment and educational opportunities in Northampton.

articles.title: WWII-era rockets found in Nantucket sand dune
articles.description:
State Police bomb technicians conducted a “controlled explosion” to neutralize the hazard.

The post WWII-era rockets found in Nantucket sand dune appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: State Police neutralized WWII-era rockets found in Nantucket sand dune on May 22, 2026.
Bomb technicians conducted a controlled explosion to ensure public safety in the area.
Residents near Nantucket can feel secure following the safe disposal of these historical munitions.
